title: Hierarchical Cross-Fitted Empirical-Bayes Encodings
summary: Driver and race IDs are high-cardinality and test contains entity shift.
rationale: Driver and race IDs are high-cardinality and test contains entity shift. Empirical-Bayes target encoding with cross-fitting can retain useful entity signal while shrinking hard toward broader parents like Race, Year, Compound, and Race×Year, reducing leakage and variance.
implementation_hint: Build fold-safe encodings such as Driver -> Driver×Compound -> Driver×RaceYear with shrinkage toward parent/global means. Fit encoders only on training folds, export OOF values for train and full-fit values for test, and combine with current physical features rather than replacing them.
expected_effect: Moderate upside if rare drivers/race combinations are currently underused or overfit by simple frequency features.
risk: Easy to leak if not fully cross-fitted; over-shrinking can wash out real entity behavior.
